metadata.statement: ScanFileFailure discriminated union is exhaustively handled: every consumer switch/match covers both 'read' and 'hydrate' variants. Within the 'hydrate' branch, the HydrationFailureReason switch covers all five values ('yaml-parse' | 'missing-field' | 'invalid-value' | 'block-parse' | 'unknown') with no fall-through. After REQ-018 (rev7), the producer mapping is fully specified: 'yaml-parse'/'missing-field'/'invalid-value' ← FrontmatterParser.parse / VO conversion (REQ-002, REQ-016 NOTE); 'block-parse' ← parseMarkdownToBlocks failure or Ok([]) (REQ-017); 'unknown' ← non-categorisable defensive fallback (REQ-018).

metadata.resolution: stages.ts:91 introduces editingSessionState aggregate; initialize-capture.ts:71 emits CaptureSessionStarted with editingSessionState. Confirmed by sprint-2 adversary review.
initialize-capture.ts:32 declares noteCreate port; line 59 invokes Note.create with empty Body. Confirmed by sprint-2 adversary review.
Option A: REQ-015 amended (Clock.now budget = 2 per run, Step 3 receives ts as arg). PROP-023 added.
scan-vault.ts:101-111 enforces NoteId regex via VO smart constructor. Confirmed by sprint-2 adversary review.
Tags narrowed to readonly Tag[]; body remains string contradicting verification-architecture.md:76-78. See FIND-013 / BEAD-069 for the residual defect.
scan-vault.ts:113-123 enforces Tag VO admissibility; new fail-grade tests for empty/whitespace tags added. Confirmed by sprint-2 adversary review.
pipeline.test.ts:55-56 introduces TS positive-membership type assertion for VaultScanned. Negative direction handled separately as FIND-012 (BEAD-068).
load-vault-config.ts:34 declares clockNow port; line 65 uses it. Confirmed by sprint-2 adversary review.
REQ-004 amended: Err(disk-full|lock|unknown) collapse to path-not-found; F-005 expanded with explicit mapping. PROP-024 added.

metadata.statement: Clock.now is called at most twice per pipeline run; hydrateFeed contains no Clock.now call and accepts no timestamp argument.
For each statDir result kind (Ok(true), Ok(false), Err(not-found), Err(permission), Err(disk-full|lock|unknown)), loadVaultConfig maps to the spec-defined reason.

metadata.statement: parseMarkdownToBlocks is pure: same Markdown input always produces identical Result with deep-equal Block tree including BlockId values (deterministic positional BlockId 'block-0..block-N-1' per Q2=A).
Per-file parseMarkdownToBlocks failure during scanVault produces failure: { kind: 'hydrate', reason: 'block-parse' }; workflow continues processing remaining files.

HydrateNote ACL is pure: (NoteFileSnapshot) → Result<Note, HydrationFailureReason> is referentially transparent. By Q2 determinism + Q5=A (no filter inside hydrateNote), the resulting Note.blocks is bit-identical to parseMarkdownToBlocks(snapshot.body).value, including BlockId values. hydrateNote does NOT filter, transform, or re-number blocks; it composes parser output with snapshot frontmatter only. A snapshot whose body triggers parseMarkdownToBlocks Err or Ok([]) returns Err('block-parse').

metadata.summary: HydrationFailureReason 'unknown' is a defensive fallback for non-categorisable hydration failures (Q3=A).

metadata.statement: REQ-018 'unknown' fallback path is reachable only via uncategorisable parser/VO errors; static REQ-002/REQ-017 producers never yield 'unknown'.

parseMarkdownToBlocks(snapshot.body) returning Ok([]) is folded by Step 2 caller to CorruptedFile.failure: { kind: 'hydrate', reason: 'block-parse' }; downstream invariant Note.blocks.length >= 1 is preserved. Per Q5=A (rev8), Ok([]) corresponds to whitespace-only body input (no content blocks); parser does NOT emit paragraph('') artifacts.
Q1=A two-call invariant: parseMarkdownToBlocks invoked exactly twice per non-corrupt file per pipeline run (Step 2 validation + Step 3 HydrateNote).
parseMarkdownToBlocks does not emit paragraph('') blocks for blank-line separators; whitespace-only body returns Ok([]).
If hydrateNote returns Err, hydrateFeed MUST throw Error('hydrateNote-invariant-violation: <filePath>: <reason>'); corruptedFiles[] MUST NOT contain Step-3 entries.

history.watchpointForSprint3: PROP-013 must be implemented as a runtime Object.keys assertion on InitialUIState (not a TypeScript type assertion which would be tautological under structural typing). The new test should assert Object.keys(initialUIState).sort() === ['corruptedFiles', 'editingSessionState', 'feed', 'tagInventory'].

FIND-012.note: Replaced tautological `_f / _t = undefined as never` with `_IsNever<T>` predicate; positive direction (vault-scanned IS in PublicDomainEvent) preserved unchanged.

The makeBody helper duplication across 3 test files matches the established pattern (each test file has its own makeVaultPath/makeTimestamp/makeNoteId per existing convention); extracting to shared module would add a new file rather than reduce code. stages.ts docstring already updated as part of the type tightening. scan-vault.ts is shorter (cast + import removed). No code smell to address.
